dataset.description: The dataset fully qualified name of the dataset. Format is [datasource]/[database]/[schema]/[table]. The exact format may vary by data source type. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#dataset-fully-qualified-name

filter.description: The SQL filter clause to be applied to the dataset (without the WHERE keyword). https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#configure-a-filter-for-all-check

variables.description: The variables to be used within the contract file for parameterization. Default values are optional. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#make-contracts-dynamic-with-variables

keys.description: Array of column names to use as keys for failed rows.

soda_agent.description: The agent configuration for remote execution via Soda Agent.

checks_schedule.description: A schedule that defines when contract's checks should be verified. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#schedule-a-contract-verification

cron.description: Cron expression in the format '* * * * *' (minute hour day month weekday). Requires string in quotes.

timezone.description: The timezone to be used for scheduled contract verification.

variables.description: The variables to be used for scheduled contract verification.

check_attributes.description: Default attributes used for all checks. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#check-attributes

reconciliation.description: Configuration for reconciliation between source and target datasets.

source.description: Source dataset configuration for reconciliation. The source dataset is compared with the main dataset.

dataset.description: The dataset fully qualified name of the source dataset. Format is [datasource]/[database]/[schema]/[table]. The exact format may vary by data source type. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#dataset-fully-qualified-name

filter.description: The SQL where filter clause to be applied to the source dataset (without the WHERE keyword).

checks.description: A list of reconciliation checks to compare source and target datasets.

row_count_diff.description: Compare row count differences between source and target datasets.

freshness_diff.description: Compare freshness differences between source and target datasets.

source_column.description: The column in the source dataset to be used for freshness comparison (typically a timestamp column).

target_column.description: The column in the target dataset to be used for freshness comparison (typically a timestamp column).

aggregate_diff.description: Compare aggregate function results between source and target datasets.

function.description: The aggregate function to be used.

source_column.description: The column in the source dataset to apply the aggregate function to.

target_column.description: The column in the target dataset to apply the aggregate function to.

duplicate_diff.description: Compare duplicate metrics between source and target datasets.

source_columns.description: The columns in the source dataset to check for duplicates.

target_columns.description: The columns in the target dataset to check for duplicates.

metric_diff.description: Compare custom metric expressions or queries between source and target datasets.

source_expression.description: The SQL expression to be used for the source dataset.

target_expression.description: The SQL expression to be used for the target dataset.

source_query.description: The SQL query to be used for the source dataset.

target_query.description: The SQL query to be used for the target dataset.

rows_diff.description: Compare individual rows between source and target datasets based on key columns. Note: source_key_columns and target_key_columns must have the same length, and source_columns and target_columns must have the same length.
rows_diff.$comment: Validation: source_key_columns and target_key_columns must have matching lengths. source_columns and target_columns must have matching lengths.

source_key_columns.description: The key columns in the source dataset for row matching. Must have the same length as target_key_columns.

target_key_columns.description: The key columns in the target dataset for row matching. Must have the same length as source_key_columns.

source_columns.description: The columns to compare in the source dataset. Must have the same length as target_columns.

target_columns.description: The columns to compare in the target dataset. Must have the same length as source_columns.

columns.description: The list of columns defining the schema of the dataset. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#columns

name.description: The name of the column as it appears in the SQL data source

description.description: The description displayed to users wherever this column is shown

data_type.description: The SQL data type as defined in the data source.

column_expression.description: SQL expression applied to the column before executing any checks on it. It may transform the value (e.g., applying a function) or extract a subfield (e.g., accessing nested data). When a check-level expression is defined, it overrides this column-level expression.

character_maximum_length.description: Maximum character length for the column. Only checked by schema validation if specified and supported by the data source.

optional.description: When set to true, the schema check will not fail if the column is missing. Default is false (column is required).

checks.description: Data quality checks for this specific column. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#columns

oneOf.description: It verifies that a specific column does not contain null or empty values beyond an acceptable threshold. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#missing-check

missing_values.description: Custom list of values to be considered missing. NULL is always considered missing.

regex.description: Regular expression to identify missing values. The regex flavor depends on the SQL engine/data source. NULL is always considered missing.

name.description: Descriptive name for the regex pattern.

oneOf.description: It verifies that the values in a column conform to a set of allowed formats, values, or constraints. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#invalid-check

valid_values.description: List of valid values. Supports all strings or numbers.

regex.description: Regular expression to identify valid values. The regex flavor depends on the SQL engine/data source.

name.description: Descriptive name for the regex pattern.

invalid_values.description: List of invalid values. Supports all strings or all numbers.

regex.description: Regular expression to identify invalid values. The regex flavor depends on the SQL engine/data source.

name.description: Descriptive name for the regex pattern.

valid_length.description: The exact length for valid values.

valid_min_length.description: The minimum length for valid values.

valid_max_length.description: The maximum length for valid values.

valid_min.description: The minimum value for valid values.

valid_max.description: The maximum value for valid values.

valid_reference_data.description: Verify that valid values exist in a reference dataset. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#invalid-check

dataset.description: The dataset fully qualified name of the reference dataset to compare against. Format is [datasource]/[database]/[schema]/[table]. The reference dataset must be in the same data source as the dataset.

column.description: The column name in the reference dataset to compare against.

oneOf.description: An Aggregate check verifies that the result of an aggregate function—such as avg, sum, min, max, or count—meets the expected thresholds. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#aggregate-check

function.description: The aggregate function to be used.

oneOf.description: It ensures that values in a column or combination of columns are unique, helping prevent data integrity issues. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#single-column-duplicate-check
Identify rows that violate a specific condition, such as a filter or SQL expression. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#failed-rows-check

expression.description: SQL expression used to identify failed rows. It is a SQL filter clause to be applied to the dataset (without the WHERE keyword).

query.description: The SQL query to be used to identify failed rows.

oneOf.description: Validate the result of a custom SQL expression or query against a threshold. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#failed-rows-check).

expression.description: The SQL expression to be used.

query.description: The SQL query to be used.

oneOf.description: Validates column values using a Large Language Model (LLM) prompt.

prompt.description: The prompt template to evaluate each value. Use {value} as placeholder.

checks.description: A list of dataset-level checks executed by Soda for contract verification. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#dataset-check

row_count.description: Verify that the dataset contains the expected number of rows.. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#row-count-check

schema.description: Validate the presence, absence, or position of columns in a dataset, or validates the data type of columns. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#schema-check

allow_extra_columns.description: Allow columns that are not defined in the contract schema.

allow_other_column_order.description: Allow columns to be in a different order than defined in the contract schema.

freshness.description: Verify if data is not too old by measuring the period between the current time and the most recent timestamp present in a given column. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#freshness-check

oneOf.description: Verify if data is not too old by measuring the period between the current time and the most recent timestamp present in a given column. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#freshness-check

column.description: The column to be used (typically a timestamp column).

duplicate.description: Ensure that values in a column or combination of columns are unique, helping prevent data integrity issues. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#duplicate-check

oneOf.description: Identify rows that violate a specific condition, such as a filter or SQL expression. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#failed-rows-check

expression.description: The SQL expression to be used.

query.description: The SQL query to be used.

oneOf.description: Validate the result of a custom SQL expression or query against a threshold. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#failed-rows-check

expression.description: The SQL expression to be used.

query.description: The SQL query to be used.

oneOf.description: Validate aggregated metrics from grouped data against thresholds. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#group-by-check

query.description: The SQL query that performs the grouping and aggregation.

fields.description: Array of field names that are used for grouping in the query.

metric_name.description: The name of the aggregated metric column in the query result to validate against the threshold.

check_qualifier.description: Required if multiple checks of the same type exist on the same object. Must be unique. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#check-qualifiers

check_filter.description: SQL expression filter applied on the metric. It is a SQL filter clause without the WHERE keyword. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#configure-a-check-filter

column_expression.description: SQL expression applied to the column before executing this check. It may transform the value (e.g., applying a function) or extract a subfield (e.g., accessing JSON data). When both column and check-level expressions are defined, the check-level expression takes precedence.

attributes.description: Optional metadata for tagging or categorizing checks. https://beta.docs.soda.io/manage-issues/check-and-dataset-attributes#attributes-in-checks

threshold.description: Threshold configuration that defines when a check passes or fails. https://docs.soda.io/soda-v4/reference/contract-language-reference#thresholds

level.description: Threshold level that determines the severity when the check fails.

metric.description: Metric unit to evaluate (count or percent).

must_be.description: Check passes if the metric equals this value.

must_not_be.description: Check passes if the metric does not equal this value.

must_be_greater_than.description: Check passes if the metric is greater than this value.

must_be_greater_than_or_equal.description: Check passes if the metric is greater than or equal to this value.

must_be_less_than.description: Check passes if the metric is less than this value.

must_be_less_than_or_equal.description: Check passes if the metric is less than or equal to this value.

must_be_between.description: Check passes if the metric is between the specified values.

must_be_not_between.description: Check passes if the metric is outside the specified values.

level.description: Threshold level that determines the severity when the check fails.

unit.description: Time unit for freshness (minute, hour, day).

must_be.description: Check passes if the metric equals this value.

must_not_be.description: Check passes if the metric does not equal this value.

must_be_greater_than.description: Check passes if the metric is greater than this value.

must_be_greater_than_or_equal.description: Check passes if the metric is greater than or equal to this value.

must_be_less_than.description: Check passes if the metric is less than this value.

must_be_less_than_or_equal.description: Check passes if the metric is less than or equal to this value.

must_be_between.description: Check passes if the metric is between the specified values.

must_be_not_between.description: Check passes if the metric is outside the specified values.

remediation.description: Remediation configuration for automated data correction.

query.description: SQL query to execute for remediation.
